faqs.question: What attracts flies to Dobbs Ferry homes and businesses?
faqs.answer: Flies are attracted to food waste, organic matter, moisture, and standing water. In Dobbs Ferry, the Hudson River proximity, outdoor dining areas along Main Street, and garbage storage areas near older buildings provide ideal conditions for fly breeding. Poor sanitation, open windows without screens, and pet waste can also contribute to fly problems.

faqs.question: How quickly can you provide fly control service in Dobbs Ferry?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day fly control services throughout Dobbs Ferry and Westchester County. Our technicians typically arrive within 24 hours of your call, and many fly problems can be resolved in a single visit. Emergency services are available for businesses like restaurants that need immediate attention.

faqs.question: What methods do you use for fly control in Dobbs Ferry?
faqs.answer: We use an integrated approach that includes identifying breeding sites, applying targeted treatments with EPA-approved products, installing fly traps where appropriate, and providing sanitation recommendations. Our methods are safe for use around children and pets, and we customize our approach based on whether you have a residential or commercial property.
